title: Lonely Asuka Endless Conflict between Needs and Wants
display_title: Lonely Asuka Endless Conflict between Needs and Wants
wikitext:
[]: Lonely Asuka - Endless Conflict between Needs and Wants             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed May 18, 2011 9:46 pm Preemptively Split from Asuka's New Plug Suit Discussion . Warren Peace wrote: Thing is, when Bardiel attacked, Asuka was in a pretty good place psychologically. Maybe as good as she'd ever been. So if there is brokenness, it has to be due to the attack.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed May 18, 2011 10:18 pm I do think that Asuka's final (dialogue) scene in 2.22 should be taken at face value. To me, there's nothing in the film to support the idea that her words should be viewed negatively. But let's go back -- people dismiss the Clockiel fight as "throwaway", but I think it's very important in her narrative. "We're lead to believe that she's accomplished everything thus far on her own, and is very capable of living without others" because events in her life have allowed her to. She takes out Clockiel literally before hitting the ground, so why value teamwork? The rest of the film is about confronting challenges bigger than herself. There's too much in (and leading up to) the conversation with Misato that contradicts the idea of regression. She inwardly accepts Rei's words (word) of thanks. Her phone call to Misato is easygoing, not contentious. She even goes so far as to point that out. To me, the moment before Bardiel attacks is an acknowledgment that it's possible to be happy and not alone.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Wed May 18, 2011 10:28 pm Warren Peace wrote: . To me, the moment before Bardiel attacks is an acknowledgment that it's possible to be happy and not alone.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed May 18, 2011 10:32 pm Warren Peace wrote: I do think that Asuka's final (dialogue) scene in 2.22 should be taken at face value. To me, there's nothing in the film to support the idea that her words should be viewed negatively.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 18, 2011 10:40 pm Yeah. She gives up on a dude who in this contuity simply didn't see her that way. That doesn't equal breaking off all contact again. She's giving up on him as a love interest, not as a friend, she's seen performing an act of friendship/doing something for him instead of demanding him to only have eyes for her while allowing herself to treat him like crap. She's grown up a bit here. (And the CR suggest its to be seen that way, as an act of kindness that makes her fate more jarring for both Shinji and the viewers) You see her smiling at Rei's words of thanks, honestly talking to Misato (Their relationship has always been rather superficial until that point) and admitting that being with others is worth it. Great emphasis is put on the "spilling your heart to others" angle. That's anything BUT a regression - Interpreting the Bardiel incident as Shinji killing her is pretty far fetched, since he refused to budge an inch rather than to risk injuring her, Gendo used the Dummyplug and Shinji quit afterwards. You may critisize Shinji's lack of action which made the use of Dummyplug necessarily in the first place, but his motivation was not to harm anyone. I don't think it is to be seen as "punishment" for anyone - It's an unforseen dramatic setback no one in the cast saw comming at the worst possible moment. (as if to say: even if you try hard, reality can sometimes be a bitch. Dun give up after the first setback) She may have realized that there are more important things to her than what she gave up on (Shinji was never top priority to her, in neither continuity. She loved him, he was important to her, but her pride always came first... he isn't that important to her if her pride comes first,. if she never gives him anything, never admits liking him, never respects him. Also, while that may be debatable, the fact remains that Rebuild!Shinji had no romantic interest in her. Shikinami never "had" him in the first place, clinging to him would've been hopeless/ would've made her unhappy - sorting out one's priorities does not equal isolating oneself - in fact, by this, she grew up/became able to think of the feelings of other people. She moved her relationship with Misato beyond the superficial level, ended her Rivalry with Rei and may have become friends with her if not for life occassionally being a bitch.In fact, she m,ay still do that in Q. )

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed May 18, 2011 10:56 pm Kendrix wrote: Yeah. She gives up on a dude who in this contuity simply didn't see her that way. That doesn't equal breaking off all contact again.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 18, 2011 11:03 pm Exept Misato says Asuka's assesment that she doesn't fit in is false and the conversation ends with "Thank you, Misato" and a complaint about the test suit. The smile was, as Misato correctly guessed, honest. It's not a smirk of triumph, it's a proper smile (As Asuka herself noted.) Anno himself wrote in the CR: "So, naturally, I received, and agreed with, a proposal memo sent out by Tsurumaki, which said, "We have to provide Shinji, as well as the audience, with the same degree of shock and sense of loss as when Touji was lost in the TV series. For that reason, by boldly portraying Asuka as a 'good character', I hope to greatly convey that sense of loss." Because of that, I wanted to have Asuka pilot Unit-03 in order to do something for Rei, and Shinji, and others - something for other people." (emphasis mine). Look it up here. Also, how does she ignore Hikari? She meets her somewhat later in the story, seems to be a bit uncertain at first, but quickly accepts her offer of eating together and we see them chatting. A bit later, she believes "It's for Hikari!" to be a believable excuse for her cooking, implying that they've become friends and Misato knows it. They just don't have that much time for a minor character like Hikari. and yet, they showed us how they met this time.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed May 18, 2011 11:08 pm Kendrix wrote: Exept Misato says Asuka's assesment that she doesn't fit in is false and the conversation ends with "Thank you, Misato" and a complaint about the test suit.

[]:             Quote Post by fadadio » Wed May 18, 2011 11:13 pm seems pretty accurate to me

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed May 18, 2011 11:13 pm You all are gonna make me break out the damn BD, aren't you? OK... Misato: "What's up, Asuka? Thoughts before the show starts?" Asuka: "I just felt like having a private chat with you for some reason." The first line in and of itself kinda disproves Sachi's thesis, in my view. If she doesn't want to connect with people, why call Misato at all? This represents a new event in her life. Evolution, not regression. Which Asuka confirms later on. Misato: "I see. I want to let you know again that I appreciate what you're doing today. Thank you." Asuka: "You don't need to thank me. It's the duty of the elite to help the ignorant masses, that's all." I think confusion about this scene might stem from the next bits of dialogue. But it's leading somewhere: Asuka: "And I'm not big on eating with everyone anyway. And it's exhausting to have to pretend you're enjoying yourself along with everyone else. And I didn't want to have to sit around watching others be happy. And all I care about is piloting the Eva. And I like being by myself to begin with. And I had no use for phony friends. And there's never been anyone who's seen me for the real me. And as long as I have the top performance scores, I can make a living at Nerv." Here the scene is broken in two. This dialogue concerns her life up to the beginning of 2.0. She's not speaking about the present , she's meditating on how things have been for her in the past. Some things have happened recently that make her think being around other people isn't all bad. How do we know? Asuka: "But some things happened recently that made me think being around other people isn't all bad. It's so not me, though." Misato: "That's not true. Because you have a kind heart." Asuka doesn't protest. Asuka: "You're the first one I've ever talked to like this. I feel better somehow. It feels nice to talk to someone. I had no idea." Misato: "This world is brimming full of fun things you have no idea about. You should enjoy it." Asuka: "Yeah, you're right. Thank you, Misato." Again, I don't see how you could read that exchange as anything but hopeful. The dialogue of this scene (along with the beautiful imagery and score) make it one of my favorite. It's a bald declaration that Asuka's come to a turning point, and is ready to experience the "brimming world of fun". And Asuka flat out agrees! Cue fan service joke, activation test chatter, and... Asuka: "I hadn't realized... I know how to laugh." She is content. If she has not conquered her fears, she is well on her way to doing so.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 18, 2011 11:18 pm That was meant to contradict your other stament, i.e, that Asuka is not being selfless here. According to this quote, she is, and that's why she has, of all sudden, gained the status of "someone important" in Shinji's book - She made a sacrifice for someone else, and that someone else was really pissed /cared about her suffering. Misato also thanks Asuka for the first time, having sort of taken her for granted until now. So does Rei. ("thank you" means a lot from her mouth.) Everyone appreciates Asuka's developement! She will keep interacting with others because she never gave up on it , I think the "It's so good to talk to ppl!" thing is said twice or thrice in that conversation.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed May 18, 2011 11:21 pm Warren Peace wrote: The first line in and of itself kinda disproves Sachi's thesis, in my view. If she doesn't want to connect with people, why call Misato at all? This represents a new event in her life. Evolution, not regression. Which Asuka confirms later on.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed May 18, 2011 11:25 pm Warren Peace wrote: Again, I don't see how you could read exchanges like the last as anything but hopeful.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 18, 2011 11:30 pm Sachi wrote: I've addressed this:

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed May 18, 2011 11:32 pm Kendrix wrote: She will keep interacting with others because she never gave up on it , I think the "It's so good to talk to ppl!" thing is said twice or thrice in that conversation.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 18, 2011 11:40 pm Sachi wrote: This can also be translated as "I've always liked being alone, and I never needed close friends," which goes along with her sentiments that all she needs to be happy is herself and her Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed May 18, 2011 11:44 pm Kendrix wrote: @ azathoth: But what happened to Asuka wasn't a consequence/punishment for her actions, it was an outer circumstance she had no influence on. Like duh, the harpies being stronger than her despite her best efforts. That does not mean she moved in the wrong direction. She'll be back and the description for that shot of her in the Q trailer reads "she can't help but smile".

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed May 18, 2011 11:44 pm Kendrix wrote: When does she say she no longer wants close relationship? She says she USED not to want them.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed May 18, 2011 11:46 pm Yeah Sachi, maybe I'm misunderstanding you, but I don't see how Asuka deciding to be a loner could be anything BUT negative. Also, what happens to Asuka after this scene is completely irrelevant. She is, of course, oblivious to Bardiel. I'm dealing with where SHE wants to go, not where life will inevitably be taking her. Asuka: "But some things happened recently that made me think being around other people isn't all bad. It's so not me, though." Misato: "That's not true. Because you have a kind heart." Sachi's argument rests on Misato being wrong, here. But I don't see why she would be. To me, Misato is speaking for Anno. (S)he's saying that it's better to embrace the world and its possibilities than to recede into your own inner life. Which I think is the entire point of Evangelion. EDIT: Damn Azathoth, you're dark Asuka is a "moron" who puts "her faith in whatever bright future she thinks she can get when she has no happiness in her present." I feel like jumping off the bridge already.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 18, 2011 11:46 pm Guys, she is socializing BY piloting Eva. ...Since when are ppl morons when unforseeable bad stuff happens to them? EVA is all about the will to live/the wonders that a better worldview can do. Give her time to react to what happened to her before judging the genuineness/worth of her epiphany.
